hardware: 1H100 ran for ~30 mins due to no credits
name: PRISM
author: Sidhant Thole
category: Learning adapters on fixed Q/K activation repartitions

primary_model.description: Two physical transformer blocks are reused as a U-Net-style encoder/decoder over 11 virtual layers. Each virtual layer has learned diagonal adapters, but Q/K activation channels remain identity.

architecture.weight_sharing: shared encoder block and shared decoder block
architecture.qk_repartition_scope: Q/K activations after W_Q/W_K projection; W_Q and W_K are not duplicated or mutated

experimentation.summary: The sweep compares a no-repartition shared-adapter control against PRISM variants that repartition Q/K activations after projection. Raw random repartitioning hurts, while adaptive blending recovers part of the loss and points to fixed 50/50 identity/permutation blending.

variants.takeaway: Best shared-weight submission in this sweep; virtual diagonal adapters provide the strongest quality/size tradeoff.
Random Q/K activation permutations add layer diversity but pay an attention alignment tax and worsen quality versus PRISM-WO.
Learned routing collapses toward a static 50/50 identity/permutation blend, partially recovering WT losses and motivating fixed-alpha structured repartition.

notes.submission_choice: PRISM-WO seed 42 is used for the top-level submission metrics because it has the best quantized BPB among the shared-weight variants in the sweep.
notes.important_precision: PRISM repartitions Q/K activations after projection, not W_Q/W_K weight matrices.
